expectations.text: Recommends Alloy, not the Docker Loki log driver
expectations.evidence: "Use Alloy. Not the Docker Loki log driver (blocks containers if Loki is down), not Fluent Bit (overkill for single-destination homelab)."

expectations.text: Explains that the Docker Loki log driver blocks container stdout when Loki is unreachable, causing containers to hang
expectations.evidence: "Not the Docker Loki log driver (blocks containers if Loki is down)" — directly states the blocking behavior.

expectations.text: Shows a discovery.docker block with host = "unix:///var/run/docker.sock" in Alloy's .alloy config syntax
expectations.evidence: Shows `discovery.docker "containers" { host = "unix:///var/run/docker.sock" }` in .alloy HCL syntax.

expectations.text: Shows a loki.source.docker block forwarding to loki.write.default.receiver
expectations.evidence: Shows `loki.source.docker "default" { ... forward_to = [loki.write.local.receiver] }` — functionally equivalent forwarding chain.

expectations.text: Mounts /var/run/docker.sock and /var/lib/docker/containers read-only into the Alloy container
expectations.evidence: Mounts `/var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro` but does NOT mount `/var/lib/docker/containers`. Only one of the two required mounts is present.

expectations.text: Recommends Alloy, not the Docker Loki log driver
expectations.evidence: Recommends Alloy as primary but then adds "Alternative (simpler): Use the Docker logging driver to ship logs directly to Loki" — explicitly recommends the Docker Loki log driver.

expectations.text: Explains that the Docker Loki log driver blocks container stdout when Loki is unreachable, causing containers to hang
expectations.evidence: The Docker log driver alternative section only says "you lose docker logs locally" — does not mention blocking container stdout when Loki is unreachable.

expectations.text: Shows a discovery.docker block with host = "unix:///var/run/docker.sock" in Alloy's .alloy config syntax
expectations.evidence: Shows `discovery.docker "containers" { host = "unix:///var/run/docker.sock" }` in .alloy HCL syntax.

expectations.text: Shows a loki.source.docker block forwarding to loki.write.default.receiver
expectations.evidence: Shows `loki.source.docker "default" { ... forward_to = [loki.write.local.receiver] }`.

expectations.text: Mounts /var/run/docker.sock and /var/lib/docker/containers read-only into the Alloy container
expectations.evidence: Mounts both `/var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro` and `/var/lib/docker/containers:/var/lib/docker/containers:ro`.

expectations.text: Identifies serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues as the root cause, not a networking or RBAC issue
expectations.evidence: "This is the #1 gotcha with kube-prometheus-stack. By default, Prometheus only discovers ServiceMonitors that have the Helm release's own labels. Your manually-created ServiceMonitor is silently ignored."

expectations.text: Specifies all three selector flags: serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues, podMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues, and ruleSelectorNilUsesHelmValues set to false
expectations.evidence: Shows all three flags under prometheus.prometheusSpec: serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues: false, podMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues: false, ruleSelectorNilUsesHelmValues: false.

expectations.text: Places the fix under prometheus.prometheusSpec in Helm values (not at the top level)
expectations.evidence: Shows `prometheus: prometheusSpec:` as the nesting path for all three flags.

expectations.text: Notes that without the fix Prometheus silently ignores manually created ServiceMonitors even though kubectl get servicemonitor shows them
expectations.evidence: "Any ServiceMonitor without those exact labels is invisible. Setting it to false makes Prometheus discover ALL ServiceMonitors in all namespaces."

expectations.text: Avoids recommending manual label matching or recreating the ServiceMonitor as the fix
expectations.evidence: Fix is entirely the three NilUsesHelmValues flags; no suggestion to add labels to the ServiceMonitor or recreate it.

expectations.text: Identifies serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues as the root cause, not a networking or RBAC issue
expectations.evidence: Identifies root cause as label mismatch (ServiceMonitor missing `release: kube-prometheus-stack` label), not the NilUsesHelmValues selector flag.

expectations.text: Specifies all three selector flags: serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues, podMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues, and ruleSelectorNilUsesHelmValues set to false
expectations.evidence: Does not mention serviceMonitorSelectorNilUsesHelmValues or any of the three selector flags anywhere in the answer.

expectations.text: Places the fix under prometheus.prometheusSpec in Helm values (not at the top level)
expectations.evidence: No Helm values fix is provided; the answer focuses on adding labels to the ServiceMonitor manifest.

expectations.text: Notes that without the fix Prometheus silently ignores manually created ServiceMonitors even though kubectl get servicemonitor shows them
expectations.evidence: Does not note the silent-ignore behavior; instead frames it as a label mismatch to debug.

expectations.text: Avoids recommending manual label matching or recreating the ServiceMonitor as the fix
expectations.evidence: Explicitly recommends adding `release: kube-prometheus-stack` label to the ServiceMonitor as the primary fix.

expectations.text: Sets repeat_interval: 4h (not a generic value like 1h or 24h)
expectations.evidence: Shows `repeat_interval: 4h` in the route configuration.

expectations.text: Uses discord_configs under the discord receiver (not a generic webhook_configs block)
expectations.evidence: Shows `discord_configs:` under the discord receiver with webhook_url field.

expectations.text: Routes severity: critical to discord and severity: warning to email as separate child routes
expectations.evidence: Shows two child routes: `match: severity: critical → receiver: discord` and `match: severity: warning → receiver: email`.

expectations.text: Keeps the webhook URL and SMTP password out of the config file — references $DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL via env substitution or webhook_url_file for k3s
expectations.evidence: Shows literal placeholder `'https://discord.com/api/webhooks/YOUR_ID/YOUR_TOKEN'` in the config. Security note says to store in Secret/env but does not show $DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL env substitution or webhook_url_file syntax.

expectations.text: Includes group_by: ['alertname', 'job'] matching the skill's reference config
expectations.evidence: Shows `group_by: ['alertname', 'job']` in the route configuration.

expectations.text: Sets repeat_interval: 4h (not a generic value like 1h or 24h)
expectations.evidence: Shows `repeat_interval: 4h` in the route configuration.

expectations.text: Uses discord_configs under the discord receiver (not a generic webhook_configs block)
expectations.evidence: Shows `discord_configs:` under both the default and discord receivers.

expectations.text: Routes severity: critical to discord and severity: warning to email as separate child routes
expectations.evidence: Shows `match: severity: critical → receiver: discord` and `match: severity: warning → receiver: email` as child routes.

expectations.text: Keeps the webhook URL and SMTP password out of the config file — references $DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL via env substitution or webhook_url_file for k3s
expectations.evidence: Shows literal placeholder webhook URL and auth_password in the config with no env substitution or webhook_url_file reference.

expectations.text: Includes group_by: ['alertname', 'job'] matching the skill's reference config
expectations.evidence: Uses `group_by: ['alertname', 'namespace']` instead of `['alertname', 'job']`.
